title: Third outage family: deploy regression / 5xx spike
description: Expand the current wedge beyond INC001 and INC002 by adding a third canonical outage family centered on a deploy regression causing 5xx spikes on a customer-facing path. This should feel like a real support-to-engineering case with deploy-window suspicion, likely owner routing, and rollback-aware framing while staying truthful about what is inferred versus validated.
implementation_tasks: add a third canonical incident across seeded and live paths with clear customer impact, likely owner, suspected deploy window, and likely regression surface
keep seeded/static and live incident semantics aligned so the new incident reads consistently across queue, incident, and training flows
add regression-specific evidence cues such as deploy timing, rollback consideration, and service ownership without overclaiming runtime validation
update the walkthrough and flagship incident references so INC003 becomes part of the core demo story
done_when: INC003 exists as a coherent third outage family across seeded and live product paths
operators can understand why the new incident is a deploy-regression class rather than a timeout or DB-pool class
the new outage family strengthens the wedge without broadening the product category

title: Bounded REPLICA and TRACE support for INC003
description: Deepen the new deploy-regression outage family so it is not just another packet shape. Add a bounded runtime pack and bounded debugging handoff for INC003 so the product can reproduce a 5xx deploy regression, compare at least one mitigation path, and hand engineering a trace-oriented debugging packet consistent with the existing truthfulness model.
implementation_tasks: add a curated runtime pack for deploy-regression replay and at least one mitigation comparison path
add bounded TRACE guidance for the likely failing request path, module, or regression window
surface runtime-backed versus inferred evidence clearly for the new outage family
verify the packaged-app runtime path for the new pack through Docker and the runtime-host relay
done_when: INC003 has bounded reproduction support consistent with the current REPLICA model
INC003 has bounded debugging guidance consistent with the current TRACE model
the product can demo a third runtime-backed outage family without breaking truthfulness

title: LLM-driven triage reasoning upgrade
description: Strengthen the triage layer so the product feels more genuinely LLM-driven and less like it is selecting from a fixed incident script. Raw logs and live incidents should produce stronger issue framing, likely subsystem, likely owner, customer impact, and next investigation steps while remaining explicit about what is LLM-inferred, memory-backed, or runtime-backed.
implementation_tasks: improve triage outputs for seeded and fresh incidents with stronger adaptive framing and uncertainty language
add or tighten a stable evidence-posture contract that distinguishes inferred, memory-backed, and runtime-backed reasoning
reduce canned-feeling summaries on fresh incidents where the current product already has enough evidence to say more
keep the stronger LLM contribution truthful and reviewable in API and UI surfaces
done_when: fresh and seeded incidents show stronger adaptive triage reasoning rather than fixed-feeling summaries
operators can see confidence and uncertainty without losing trust in the product
the product remains explicit about what is inferred versus validated

title: Agent deliberation visibility and reasoning timeline
description: Make the multi-agent investigation workflow easier to understand by showing what each agent contributed and which evidence shifted the case. The goal is not to expose raw chain-of-thought, but to make the operator and buyer experience feel like a real coordinated investigation rather than a stack of static panels.
implementation_tasks: define a safe, concise reasoning-timeline or contribution contract for SENTINEL, PRISM, REPLICA, TRACE, FORGE, and GUARDIAN
render the agent contribution flow in the incident surface with clear turning points and evidence references
make the timeline work for seeded incidents, runtime-backed incidents, and fresh live incidents
update demo-facing docs so presenters can explain the agent interaction cleanly
done_when: operators can see what each agent contributed and why the case evolved
the product feels more like a coordinated agent system and less like a static report
the reasoning visibility remains concise and truthful rather than speculative

title: Pilot conversion kit for the three-outage wedge
description: Turn the expanded three-outage wedge into a stronger pilot conversation package. The product and supporting docs should make it easy to explain outage-family coverage, runtime-backed support state, engineering handoff quality, and why this wedge is commercially useful before any broader platform ambition is attempted.
implementation_tasks: update buyer-proof, training, and presentation surfaces for the three supported outage families
tighten the operator and buyer narrative around where NEXUS is runtime-backed versus inference-first
strengthen demo and onboarding materials so the pilot motion is easier to repeat without founder-only explanation
keep all pilot-conversion claims aligned with measured or honestly estimated product evidence
done_when: the three-outage wedge is easy to explain to buyers and pilot users
training and proof surfaces reflect the expanded support state cleanly
the product is more ready for real pilot conversations without changing the category story

title: Final wow-effect UI completion across core surfaces
description: Finish the remaining premium product polish so the core surfaces feel presentation-ready and visually intentional. The pass should focus on intake, incident, training, and handoff/export surfaces, with stronger hierarchy, continuity, and motion while preserving readability and evidence truthfulness.
implementation_tasks: complete the premium visual pass on the most buyer-visible product surfaces
improve hierarchy, spacing, motion, and continuity for intake, incident, training, and export flows
make the strongest demo path feel more impressive without making it less legible or less honest
keep desktop and mobile presentation quality within the existing product shell
done_when: the product feels materially more polished on the key demo and buyer-facing screens
the wow-effect pass is visibly complete rather than partially landed
the visual changes strengthen presentation quality without weakening workflow comprehension

title: Post-85 wedge-strengthening checkpoint and control-doc refresh
description: Close the post-85 wedge-strengthening phase with a deliberate checkpoint. Confirm the product now supports three flagship outage families, stronger LLM-driven triage, clearer agent visibility, stronger pilot proof, and a finished UI polish pass while remaining bounded and truthful.
implementation_tasks: run the full validation stack across tests, browser flows, demo flow, and packaged-app Docker path
refresh owner-facing docs so the new wedge strength, boundaries, and pilot readiness are current
refresh control docs so the next frontier is accurate and the completed backlog is no longer treated as active
write a concise checkpoint summary before any broader post-92 roadmap work starts
done_when: the repo ends the phase with a clean validated checkpoint
owner-facing docs clearly describe the three-outage wedge and stronger LLM triage posture
control docs are current and ready for the next planning phase
